explanation: Conversation topics have drifted from the stated objective
conversation_text: Hey B, so I was looking at our budget review like you asked. Last year we spent $50k on marketing, right? That's our anchor, so I'm thinking we stick to $50k again for this year. No need to overthink it. Yeah, uh, $50k sounds familiar. But didn't we get way fewer leads last year? I mean, maybe we should bump it up a bit? Or... nah, you're right, last year's number is solid. Exactly! Anchoring to last year keeps it simple. For salaries, we had $200k total. Everyone's happy, no complaints, so let's lock that in at $200k. Easy peasy. Hmm, true, but inflation? Things cost more now. Wait, did we even adjust last year? Oh well, $200k it is then. What about office supplies? Last year was $5k, I think. Spot on, $5k. We didn't run out or anything, so why change? Oh, and rent—$30k last year. Landlord didn't hike it, so $30k again. Prevents any fresh math headaches.
